title: lippia hirsuta var. vernonioides
intro: Lippia hirsuta var. vernonioides, also known as Mexican marigold, is a plant species in the verbena family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lippia hirsuta var. vernonioides include its use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Lippia hirsuta var. vernonioides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
